programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A crazed vigilante obsessed with Walker hits town and starts causing trouble

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A daredevil driver is decapitated while performing a stunt at a street party, bringing the festivities to a premature end. Realising the incident was murder rather than an accident, the team focuses the investigation on a bystander whose expertise with remote-controlled cars makes him a likely suspect. David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An explosion at an apartment building brings navy officer Terence Keith to the team's attention and it transpires he had been on medication for anxiety, a condition that had led to him being ejected from a pilot's training course. Ducky's profile of Keith suggests that he may be unstable and about to exact revenge for having his dream of becoming a fighter ace taken away from him, and evidence points to him intending to carry out a suicide attack

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Ziva's family farm in Israel is devastated by an explosion, and the former NCIS agent is reported dead. Determined to seek justice, Tony ignores orders to stay off the case and sets out to track down Trent Kort, the British national and ex-CIA agent who remains the prime suspect

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: When Kimble asks a university law professor whether a new trial would help him, the professor has his students stage a mock trial using the real transcript of the case

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: An actress hires Perry to look into a man claiming to be her long-lost son, but when a film critic is killed, he must defend her against a murder charge as well

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: While decommissioning an old nuclear missile, a simple accident exposes the team to toxic vapours while trying to prevent it from detonating

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker faces a race against time to save the life of a man sentenced to death row for a murder he did not commit. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Agent Callen poses as an arms trade contact in a bid to catch killer Michael Aaron Rivkin. Meanwhile, Tony fears Ziva may not be loyal to the team, and a psychologist uncovers the truth about the relationship between Gibbs and Agent Macy. With Chris O'Donnell, Louise Lombard and LL Cool J

programme.desc: Sam investigates the background of an eastern European diplomat accused of a violent assault on a prostitute and learns he could be a murderer. programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mrs Slocombe alarms the staff by announcing she is expecting a happy event - but little do they realise it is merely her cat that is pregnant. Classic comedy, starring John Inman, Frank Thornton and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Foggy tries to implement a new fitness regime, but Clegg is reluctant to take part and insists some of the world's nicest people are out of shape. Brian Wilde stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo tracks down his missing ferret to Nora's washing basket, but smuggling it out unnoticed proves tricky. Bill Owen and Kathy Staff star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: It's 1969 and six months since the shootings in Durham Cathedral, and while Gently is almost back to full fitness, Bacchus has lost his confidence and tenders his resignation. Gently is determined to help his sergeant, so he gets him involved in one last case, the death in custody of a man arrested during a street protest - an investigation that leaves both detectives questioning what it means to be a police officer at a time when attitudes to the force are changing. Martin Shaw and Lee Ingleby star, with Lisa McGrillis, Steve Evets and Ruth Gemmell

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The gun used to kill a boxer is discovered at the scene of an armed robbery 15 years later, prompting the team to reinvestigate the case. Twenty-one-year-old Eddie Mayfair was shot dead a week after winning an important bout, which left his opponent seriously injured and unable to carry on his career. The detectives initially suspect revenge as a motive, but the case is complicated when they uncover the rivalries and money-making aspects of the sport. Guest starring Maggie O'Neill (Shameless) and Chloe Howman (Casualty)

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A Sudanese politician vows to go on hunger strike until the UK returns the skull of an important 19th-century ruler to his country. While searching for the article, Boyd is asked to re-open the case of an Iraqi shopkeeper murdered in front of his daughter 20 years ago. A tip-off from a Foreign Office official gives the team a lead - and as it probes further, it seems the two investigations could be connected. Trevor Eve stars

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Sid attempts to install a doorbell in his cafe, so Compo, Clegg and Foggy try to assist - with disastrous consequences. John Comer, Peter Sallis, Brian Wilde and Bill Owen star in Roy Clarke's long-running comedy

programme.desc: Foggy feels the need to do something special for the Queen's Silver Jubilee so he volunteers himself, Clegg, and Compo to help with the town's pageant. However, the need arises to find someone capable of driving a tractor. Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen star

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Havers and Lynley visit Cambridge to investigate the murder of Elena Weaver, a respected lecturer's deaf daughter. programme.desc: Retired DI Frank Patterson presents the team with information regarding the getaway car used in a 30-year-old safety deposit-box robbery. Phil Daniels guest stars

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Two long-dead sexually conjoined bodies fall through the ceiling of a former bank after being disturbed by builders, and a gold ingot found at the scene suggests a connection with another cold case. Meanwhile, the friendship between Margaret and Bob Bishop arouses suspicion. Richard Griffiths and Maggie Steed star

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Siegfried takes matters into his own hands when he discovers who shot war veteran Jacob Pearson's dog, and a farmer is found to be sneaking his cows onto a neighbouring property to enjoy better grazing. Christopher Timothy and Robert Hardy star

programme.title: Brush Strokes
programme.desc: Jacko is unable to repel Lesley's unwanted advances and turns to a newly single Sandra for advice. Meanwhile, Veronica has an argument with Lionel. Comedy, starring Karl Howman

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: The staff come up with a most unusual plan to celebrate young Mr Grace's birthday. Department store sitcom, starring John Inman, Trevor Bannister, Wendy Richard and Mollie Sugden

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg attends the funeral of an old friend, and later tries to convince Foggy and Compo that flowers have emotions and that they react to vocal tone and music. Compo's attention is more focused on his own needs, in particular enacting a new plan to drive Nora Batty into his arms. Peter Sallis, Bill Owen and Brain Wilde star

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Compo and Clegg gatecrash Foggy's trip to see a female friend in Wales. Comedy, starring Brian Wilde, Peter Sallis and Bill Owen

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: A doctor is accused of murdering a seriously ill patient with a morphine overdose, a case which echoes a dilemma in Deed's personal life and sets him on the path to potential ruin. Meanwhile, Charlie's HIV-positive friend finds herself in danger of losing her baby to Social Services, and calls on the judge for help. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The veteran detectives investigate the murder of a palaeontologist at the Natural History Museum, discovering the victim was a respected but outspoken scientist with a knack for rubbing people up the wrong way. When it comes out he was strongly opposed to the museum's sponsorship deal with a large fuel company, the cops soon have plenty of suspects. Amanda Redman stars, with guest appearances by Trevor Bannister, Lucy Brown and Natasha Little

programme.title: Waking the Dead
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The team reinvestigates the work of serial killer James Jenson when the wallets of his victims surface on the day he is released from a secure psychiatric unit. However, after turning the spotlight on a second suspect, Dr Bruno Rivelli, and the children's home he previously ran, they find themselves with another murder case on their hands

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Stuart impresses his Sun Hill colleagues as he makes his debut on TV crime show Wanted, hoping to clear up a burglary case during which the victim, lawyer Ian Stroud, suffered a massive heart attack. The public response is overwhelming, leaving the team to pick through a mountain of false leads - including a sighting of a man once defended in court by Stroud who served eight years for a rape he did not commit

programme.title: Mercy Street
programme.desc: Lisette re-creates on paper the face of a disfigured, amnesiac soldier, leading to a family reunion. When Anne's plans to be made head nurse fail, she rejoins Hale to undermine McBurney's authority. Pinkerton questions the Greens, causing a rift

Noni's best friend Malan and Deniz's sister Ekin are also full of hope as their plans to have a child together become possible, with Deniz agreeing to be a donor so he can be the father of their child. The future looks bright until Noni sees a ghost from her past - her husband who was killed in a terrible fire in Edinburgh, or at least man who looks very much like him. Drama, starring Gwyneth Keyworth, Richard Harrington and Memet Ali Alabora

programme.title: Y Ty Rygbi
programme.desc: Rhys ap William, Shane Williams, Sioned Harries and Mike Phillips look forward to the Six Nations Championship

programme.title: Drych
programme.desc: A film following the Head of Radio 1, Aled Haydn Jones and his partner Emile, as they embark on an emotional journey to have a baby biologically with the help of their surrogate, Dawn

programme.title: Sgwrs Dan y Lloer
programme.desc: Elin Fflur visits actor and marathon runner Mark Lewis Jones in the garden of his home in Cardiff, hearing about a very colorful career and life history

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits property developments in Wolverhampton and South Norwood, south-east London, while Martin Roberts looks round a three-bedroom semi-detached in Derby

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: The Parkers dream of balancing a new business venture with family time in the great outdoors, so sample a week in Dorset to try to tempt their grown-up children away from London

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: In Essex, police officers are doused in petrol and threatened with being set alight after a moped chase ends in mayhem. In Knutsford, Cheshire, an officer trying to catch a suspected drug dealer falls onto a train track, sustaining life-changing injuries.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion looks at the history of the East Grinstead Potter

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie is hungry for the truth, while Karen hopes Tom and Ella have turned a corner. Ruhma encounters a reluctant mother and her complicated family

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Max and Miranda go on a Wild West adventure to the horse-riding world of Rancho del Ray to investigate a credit card scam, only to discover a tragic history that leads to murder. Crime drama, starring Elen Rhys and Julian Looman

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah helps a recently retired Essex couple to look for a home in Staffordshire on their budget of £550,000, hoping to find a detached property with a view. Sonali also visits a celebrated Victorian pottery that is now a a hub of art, design and training

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Four groups of dedicated farmers who have shown resilience and ingenuity, go up against each other bidding to win this year's Against the Odds accolade at the Annual British Farming Awards. Narrated by Denis Lawson

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presents the programme in which five dealers bid against one another to buy a pedal car, an Edwardian card table, a quirky voice box and a mystery pot

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Ronan Keating present the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Bournemouth for young married couple Thomas and Rhiann. They have a budget of £3,000 to create a shady, flower-filled space in their sunny back garden and Tom has a lifelong dream of putting in a massive koi carp pond

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise's disappearance leaves her family and friends concerned, especially Chelsea, who is in a state of panic. Max babysits for Abi, causing him to reminisce about his much-missed daughter and how his life has not worked out the way he would have wanted. Dotty pushes Kush to decide whether he wants the spare room in her house, while the call centre has a new starter - Big Mo

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Sara Pascoe, Dan Walker, Nicola Coughlan and Mr Motivator to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The competition reaches the halfway point, where the contestants are challenged by Mary Berry to prepare the ultimate Sunday roast. The only rule is that the meal must involve roasted meat and some form of potatoes - they are otherwise free to do whatever they want. Two cooks compete to produce the best pork crackling, while another adds a Morrocan twist. For the second task, Chris Bavin challenges the cooks to make banana bread without using flour, while the final round involves a classic French dessert

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The presenting skills of the contestants are tested as they host their own daytime magazine show Morning Glory, with Lorraine Kelly joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by star of stage and screen Felicity Kendal, who is making her musical theatre debut in Anything Goes. Brit Regé-Jean Page, star of the hit Netflix drama Bridgerton, also appears as does rock legend Dave Grohl, who is promoting the new Foo Fighters album Medicine at Midnight. Derry Girls actress Siobhan McSweeney idscusses becoming host of The Great Pottery Throw Down and comedian Alan Carr reflects on taking the helm as host of Interior Design Masters. Plus, singer and podcaster Jessie Ware performs her new single Remember Where You Are

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits Dover to view a commercial property with scope to convert into residential use. Martel Maxwell heads to a house in Orpington, and Dion Dublin is in Liverpool

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Chris Bavin helps a woman who wants to find a coastal retirement home in East Sussex, but her husband argues that she will regret moving away from her grandchildren

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: A shocking discovery by two unarmed police officers brings a bomb disposal unit to a suburb of Merseyside. In Kent, a criminal driver swerves to drive directly at a police officer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is joined by John Cameron and Gary Pe in Malvern, Worcestershire, and a regular Bargain Hunt expert talks about his love of Whitefriars Glass

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Karen has trouble in the kitchen, while things bubble up with Emma and Aashiq. Rob has to help a pensioner who thinks he's a secret agent

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max investigate the disappearance of a champion cyclist while Ines is away - a case that inspires a little sporting rivalry between the two detectives. When the missing man's wife receives a ransom demand, evidence merges of a complex situation involving mistaken identity and murder

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps two retired police officers to find a new home in North Wales with at least an acre of land, and also tries one of the region's many extreme water sports

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Darren and Emma are preparing for their first-ever cattle show, but first they have to beat seasoned breeders Grania and Simon at their local Launceston Show

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: A group of dealers bid against each other for items collected by the public including two teddies, an Islamic tile, a painting and a chair brought in by a 12-year-old boy

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v West Ham United (Kick-off 7.30pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the tie at Old Trafford, as the teams contest a place in the last eight. United prevailed 3-2 here against rivals Liverpool in the previous round, with Bruno Fernandes coming off the bench to score the winner. The Hammers' progress was considerably more serene, as they recorded a 4-0 victory at home to League One side Doncaster Rovers. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Micah Richards

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the pandemic's ongoing impact on performing arts in the UK. Featuring footage shot throughout 2020 documenting the ways people and organisations have strived to keep the industry alive, from ballet and opera to comedy clubs and live music venues. The film explores innovative new approaches to performance, from drive-in opera to world premiere dance pieces, as well as examining how the wider political backdrop of current events has shaped contemporary art. With contributions from Carlos Acosta, Tamara Rojo, James Graham, Matthew Warchus, Roderick Williams and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Musicals: The Greatest Show
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith introduces a celebration of the world of musical theatre from the London Palladium, with the host joining Amanda Holden to perform I Know Him So Well from Chess. There are also numbers from The Greatest Showman, Mamma Mia!, Hamilton, Hairspray, Funny Girl, The Phantom of the Opera and Dreamgirls, while international superstar Idina Menzel talks to West End legend Elaine Paige

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the progress of office space in Tring, Hertfordshire, a house in Ashton-under-Lyne in Lancashire and a semi-detached in Ambergate, Derby, after they are sold at auction

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: Alex Mearns contemplates leaving life in suburbia behind and relocating to Cornwall, but his wife Robyn has reservations about leaving their families behind

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: In Humberside, firefighters are shocked when they are attacked by the residents of a burning house as they try to put out the fire

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Tim Weeks and John Cameron help two teams find items to sell for a profit at auction at the Kent County Showground. Charlie Ross pays a visit to Leeds Castle

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Daniel has to deal with a counter fraud investigator who has his sights on ghost patients. Meanwhile, Lily has a surprise for Al

programme.title: The Mallorca Files
programme.desc: Miranda and Max attempt to find a masked assailant who stole the famous Icon of St Nicholas, but the escape of a person of interest results in a furious Ines removing the pair from the case, causing a rift between the two detectives. When the icon suddenly reappears, Miranda and Max must put their differences aside as they are drawn into a furious cat-and-mouse chase

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a retired couple find a characterful property in Norfolk, as well as learning about boat building in Wells-next-the-Sea and sailing a traditional yacht

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two inner-city London charities head to the 46th Lambeth Country Show. This is their chance to raise awareness, funds and support so they can continue their vital community work

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a collection of curiosities, an unusual chair, a mask, candlesticks from a famous maker, and a writing slope. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a clock made from the propeller of a First World War plane, a pair of stained glass windows, and an ancient shoe-stretching machine

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max reaches out to an unlikely source, causing him to break down, clearly unable to cope. Kathy feels lost with Ian still missing, so Bobby suggests she sees a doctor. A desperate Shirley and Mick learn that a woman matching Tina's description has been spotted and decide to take matters into their own hands

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha gets the shock of his life when a dangerous and vulnerable Jodie appears back on the scene. Kian's new boss Sahira breezes back into Holby, much to everyone's surprise. An outgoing new nurse has her first day working in a busy AAU, which proves to be a trial by fire

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: In the quarter-final, the remaining celebrities show off their celebration cakes for Mary Berry's Ultimate Challenge. They also take part in Chris Bavin's all-vegetarian Rustle Up, tasked with making the humble chickpea the star of their dishes, in a bid to avoid Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator. Presented by Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from six of the fifth-round matches, including Everton v Tottenham Hotspur, Swansea City v Manchester City and Manchester United v West Ham United. Manchester City have once again avoided Premier League opposition in their quest to reach the quarter-finals, leaving much of the attention of the matches at Goodison Park and Old Trafford, which featured four sides in the top half of the top flight. Plus, highlights of Leicester City v Brighton & Hove Albion, Sheffield United v Bristol City and Burnley v Bournemouth

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel are still in with a chance of making the play-offs, but their last few matches are critical as they face off against some of the toughest teams in the league. Can the players, coaches Joey and Fabian rise to the challenge and prove they deserve to be in the top six? Patrik opens up about his issues with weight, and reveals that his parents have never come to see him play League of Legends.

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ashes-winning captain Michael Vaughan, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, world champion speed-skater Elise Christie and Great Britain sprint great John Regis join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Raj Bisram help two teams look for items at Bath Racecourse, while presenter Eric Knowles looks at surgical equipment from the past, courtesy of the Bath Medical Museum

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Matt, Dani and Murray in the Lake District await the arrival of over 1,100 lambs in six weeks, but some early lambs make an unscheduled appearance. Meanwhile in Northumberland, shepherdess Emma Gray and firefighter husband Ewan are preparing for the Whitebred Shorthorn pedigree bull sale in Carlisle, and in Cumbria, father and daughter team Frank and Georgia are nearing the end of the birthing season for their 120 goats

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright demonstrates exercises designed to get muscles moving

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: The start of Shakespeare Week, plus Pythagoras in maths, and a special look at the NHS

programme.title: Shakespeare Unlocked
programme.desc: Actors and directors from the Royal Shakespeare Company explore key scenes from the play, with Jonathan Slinger as Macbeth and Aislín McGuckin as his wife

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: Historian Ruth Goodman and archaeologists Peter Ginn and Tom Pinfold turn back the clock to run a farm at the Weald & Downland Open Air Museum in West Sussex as it would have been done in 1500 under the reign of Henry VII. Peter and Tom move the sheep to fresh grass, build a pigsty with no nails and plough with a pair of cows, while Ruth makes tallow lights out of rushes and sheep fat, and performs the ritual of laying the table - an act full of Christian symbolism. The team also celebrates Palm Sunday, one of the most important religious events of the year, with Peter dressing up to play the role of a prophet

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and Anna Haugh discuss food served at royal birthday celebrations, including a venison with juniper enjoyed by Queen Elizabeth II for her 80th

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers defeat rival gang the Zappas in a fight over territory, while Flower gives birth to another litter. However, tragedy strikes, and a single act threatens to change the face of the manor for ever

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Catherine Southern are in Margam Country Park in south Wales to value items brought in by the public. Mark meets someone with an eye for a bargain, while Catherine is taken by a wonderful pink Rolls-Royce. Presenter Paul Martin also take the opportunity to visit the birthplace of one of his heroes - a Welshman who took Hollywood by storm

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kae Kurd, Zoe Lyons, Andrew Hunter Murray and Kate Robbins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The chef demonstrates how to make healthy dishes full of exciting flavours and textures, including Spanish-style squid and a chickpea and chorizo stew. Meanwhile, the group are invited to take part in a martial arts session, before Bev persuades the gang to try out her favourite workout, a vigourous dance class called Clubbercise. Later, Tom invites the whole gang over for lunch, where he prepares an easy chicken tray-bake, full of aromatic Moroccan flavours

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair, asking them questions on the specialist subjects of the Mongol invasions, the plays of Aristophanes, the rise of the East India Company, and Broadway musicals of the 1940s and '50s, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell asks the questions as the Whodunnits take on the Corkscrews in the first of the quarter-final matches, with a place in the semi-finals at stake. They must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between groups of four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Roland-Garros, Chicago O'Hare, Lindy Hop, and Orville the Duck

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In the fifth of the quarter-finals, two more teams of four students compete to make it to the next stage of the competition as they try to win two matches and progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The fishing grounds of St Ives Bay have supported the fleets of two ports for generations, but now change threatens the future of the fishermen of Hayle. A vast new development with hundreds of homes and leisure facilities is under construction, and the fishermen fear that their days working from their compound next to it may be numbered

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Citizen Khan star Adil Ray hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz from November 2019, with comedian Ivo Graham and journalist Helen Lewis joining captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the news

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: NFL Highlights
programme.desc: Tampa Bay Buccaneers v Kansas City Chiefs. Action from American football's showpiece match, which took place at Raymond James Stadium in Tampa, Florida

programme.title: Alastair Campbell: Depression and Me
programme.desc: The journalist and former Downing Street press secretary - best known as Tony Blair's 'spin doctor' during his tenure as prime minister - talks about his lifelong battle with depression. Encouraged by his family, Alastair sets out on a journey to explore if cutting-edge science can offer him, and the millions of people like him, the hope of one day living free of the illness. As he tries to better understand his depression, he also reflects on key events in his life and asks if they could have had a negative effect on his mind

programme.title: This World
programme.desc: The second part of this two-part documentary explores the initial outbreak of Covid-19 in the US. When Covid-19 first arrived on American soil, US federal health authorities were seemingly unaware of how transmissible the virus truly was. This account chronicles 54 days in which the United States made crucial decisions that would severely impact the spread of the virus. Featuring interviews with public health officials and scientists who had already concluded that the virus had global pandemic potential, who were confronted with a failing diagnostic test and a president who appeared to repeatedly undermine the true threat of the virus

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison discovers how communities on her home turf of Gloucestershire are pulling together to one another during the pandemic. She meets the producers on a mission to get everyone eating local and healthy meals at affordable prices, finds out why gardening is on prescription, and helps out with the endangered hedgehog, which is facing a precarious future. Wildlife film-maker Jack Perks gets up close to dippers, Adam Henson sizes up a new boar for his pigs and Charlotte Smith investigates what future trade deals could mean for the food on the nation's plates

programme.title: Inside Culture with Mary Beard
programme.desc: The classicist returns with the cultural review show, and hot on the heels of Joe Biden's inauguration, she talks to Veep and The Thick of It creator Armando Iannucci to ask how comedians and satirists adjust to a new regime. She is also joined by David Olusoga, who recently interviewed Barack Obama, to explore succession through the ages

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham is live in the New Forest, looking for Dartford warblers, and Iolo Williams is looking at the water shrews and discovering their amazing ability to survive the winter. Plus, Megan McCubbin presents the first of a four-part look at species that aren't as loved as they should be, starting with rats.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: CCTV footage from a petrol station helps convict a gang of cold-blooded killers. Elsewhere, a criminal's mobile phone finally uncovers a gang targeting the vulnerable

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wanted: A Simple Life
programme.desc: The Parkers dream of balancing a new business venture with family time in the great outdoors, so sample a week in Dorset to try to tempt their grown-up children away from London

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: The dealers bid against each other on a childhood Viewmaster collection, a set of art deco candle holders and an apothecary box. Presented by Nigel Havers

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about the county's fishing communities, beginning in the popular tourist resort of Newquay as restrictions begin to ease after the coronavirus lockdown. It is not a moment too soon for the town's fishermen, most of whom have been tied up for months, and with lost time to catch up on, and livelihoods on the line, the fleet is steaming into a summer like no other

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright brings fitness to secondary school children, as he leads a workout session designed to increase strength

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Shakespeare's use of language. Plus, a guide to maps and a look at how photosynthesis works in plants

programme.title: Romeo and Juliet: Royal Shakespeare Company
programme.desc: Erica Whyman directs the 2018 RSC production of Shakespeare's most famous story of love at first sight. Starring Bally Gill and Karen Fishwick in the title roles

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers attempt to come to terms with the loss of an important member, and Flower's daughter Rocket Dog takes charge of the gang, but struggles to settle into her new role

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Zaphod leaves the Whiskers due to the imminent change in season. However, as he sets out to make his own way in the world, he is confronted by an old adversary

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team are at Highcliffe Castle in Dorset where Christina Trevanion and Philip Serrell select items including a 1960s coat by Biba and a Mouseman cheeseboard

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kae Kurd, Zoe Lyons, Andrew Hunter Murray and Kate Robbins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's tour brings him to Perigord, which is famous for its black truffles and walnuts, as well as duck dishes. He pays a visit to his favourite market in the city of Perigueux, and traces the historic connections between England and the city, which was once known as the Kingdom of Aquitaine. Inspired by his visit, he returns to the kitchen to prepare Enchaud, a traditional pot roast pork from the region, before heading towards the shores of the Mediterranean and the Cote Catalan near the Spanish border

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Michelle Ogundehin splits the contestants into three teams, each of which is given an open-plan office space to transform for a technology client, a sustainable beauty brand and a media team. The brief is to create a work space, a break-out space and a meeting space within the office, and Michelle will be looking for both displays of signature style and work that suits the needs of the client

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Investigators examine the home of a man covered in blood who hands himself into the police declaring he has murdered his wife, but who also claims to have no memory of the incident. Crime scene coordinator Jo Ward carries out analysis of the property in the hope of piecing together a sequence of events, but a crack in the man's mobile provides an insight into his motives

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by a selection of celebrity guests

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The globetrotting presenter looks back on some of the most incredible wildlife stories he has encountered, from a giant tortoise sanctuary in the Seychelles to armed conservationists defending a forest in Belize. Catching up with old friends, Simon discovers how one of his programmes help protect a crucial whale sanctuary off the coast of Australia

programme.title: Katie Price: Harvey and Me
programme.desc: Model and businesswoman Katie Price's son Harvey was born with a rare disorder that affects brain function, hormones and vision, and he has a long list of medical and behavioural conditions that mean his future is unpredictable. This documentary follows Katie and Harvey as he turns 18, with the pair exploring the options of further education colleges and meeting other families that contain disabled young adults

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham is live in the New Forest, looking at what's been happening in the badger sett and at the latest action from the River Ness. Elsewhere, Gillian Burke is at RSPB Old Moor, where she explores the life-cycle of the eel, and Megan McCubbin goes swimming with jellyfish in a bid to understand why they are misunderstood

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: How a discarded gun cartridge assisted police in convicting a man who had gunned down a fellow officer. Plus, the packet of medication that helped lock up a gang of violent jewellery thieves. Narrated by Chuku Modu

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Nick Hall and Ochuko Ojiri lead two teams competing at Ardingly in West Sussex, while presenter Christina Trevanion looks at the history of the East Grinstead Potter

programme.title: Incredible Journeys with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon Reeve presents highlights of his journeys around the world, and catches up with the people he met, including a Burmese undercover human rights campaigner, a homeless woman living in a Hollywood railway bridge, and a 10 year-old boy he met working in terrible conditions in a glass factory in Bangladesh

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: Mark Wright brings fitness to secondary school children, this time focusing on routines that get hearts pumping

programme.title: Politics Live

programme.title: Bitesize Daily for Secondary
programme.desc: Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet and King Lear are dissected. Plus, chemistry and French

programme.title: Shakespeare's Mother: The Secret Life of a Tudor Woman
programme.desc: Historian and broadcaster Michael Wood charts the story of Mary Arden, the mother of William Shakespeare. Born during the reign of Henry VIII, she was the daughter of a Warwickshire farmer, and found herself part of the burgeoning middle class in Stratford-upon-Avon following her marriage to wool and hide dealer John Shakespeare. Together, they had eight children - three of whom died young - and her husband went on to become mayor before he was declared bankrupt, and they faced financial ruin, religious persecution and power politics - until they were rescued by their eldest son William

programme.title: Tudor Monastery Farm
programme.desc: The team turns its attention to wool, which was the source of much of the country's wealth in Tudor times and had monasteries at the centre of its international trade. Peter Ginn builds a shearing bench, Tom Pinfold makes a salve out of broom, suet, brine and urine to seal any wounds that might occur while shearing, and Ruth Goodman uses sheep's milk to produce cheese to sell at market

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk celebrates royal food inspired by the days of India and Empire, as Paul Ainsworth cooks up quail and potato curry, said to be a favourite of Queen Victoria. Plus, historian Dr Annie Gray visits Osborne House on the Isle of Wight where Victoria, Empress of India, showcased her grand passion for the raj, and even hired her own Indian chefs

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The Whiskers have to protect their pups from a deadly enemy, while Mozart and her sister Kinkajoo are forced to depend on one another for survival. Bill Nighy narrates

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from the show, with visits to Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset and Powderham Castle in Devon

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Kae Kurd, Zoe Lyons, Andrew Hunter Murray and Kate Robbins go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts a quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick's journey takes him to the Spanish border and the Mediterranean coast. He discovers how Catalan and French influences have shaped the culinary legacy of Roussillon in the foothills of the Pyrenees, and samples some of the area's famous mountain cuisine, including grilled sausages, snails and fresh fish caught in a self-contained seawater lake - all with generous servings of the region's famous garlic mayonnaise. Back in his country kitchen, Rick prepares pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Just as the annual lambing season gets into full swing, coronavirus grips the nation, and the farmers must work hard to keep their businesses afloat. In Aberdeenshire, new entrant farmers Isla and George lose their childcare, meaning Isla is left to look after the livestock and farm shop with three young children in tow. Elsewhere in the Lake District, Matt and Dani are faced with challenges when the usual team of students they rely on during their busiest time of year can no longer help with the calving

programme.title: Trump Takes on the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. The first edition looks back over Trump's first year and a half in office, when he fell out spectacularly with some of America's oldest allies, and offers an inside look at crucial meetings and summits where the US President shocked his counterparts

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Phil Wang comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by star of The Mash Report Rachel Parris and actor, voiceover artist and stand-up Brennan Reece

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, with Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Investigators examine the home of a man covered in blood who hands himself into the police declaring he has murdered his wife, but who also claims to have no memory of the incident. Crime scene coordinator Jo Ward carries out analysis of the property in the hope of piecing together a sequence of events, but a crack in the man's mobile provides an insight into his motives

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson makes macaroni cheese with a sauce containing brown crab, gruyere and Aleppo pepper, and prepares chicken with orzo that owes a lot to one of her mother's recipes. She also demonstrates chocolate cookies that can be made in an instant when the need for something sweet is great.

programme.title: A Perfect Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a look at how Earth's five oceans connect to form the largest ecosystem on the planet, and how its network of currents supports the health of the seas and marine wildlife. Cameras follow vast super-pods of dolphins and the flocks of gannets that follow them as they scour the oceans searching for food, as well as the tiny yet dazzling flamboyant cuttlefish that's looking for a mate

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and the team present an edition focusing on wellbeing for both humans and animals, and how sometimes nature can have the answers. Megan McCubbin looks at the deep intelligence of pigeons, and why they should be admired, and there's a report from a woman who goes wild swimming with dolphins, something that has proved of enormous benefit to her mental health. Iolo Williams is at the Centre for Alternative technology in Wales and Chris rounds up the latest footage from the cameras in the New Forest

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred goes to Buckingham Palace to collect his MBE. Before his royal appointment, he makes a detour to the Crossness Pumping Station, in east London, where volunteers spent 18 years restoring four great beam engines, which are possibly the world's largest

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to create a deep forest with tall majestic trees, rain puddles and dark fresh air

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Victoria and Albert curator Keith brings the original Frankenstein's monster out of the archives for a sci-fi exhibition, including the clothes worn by actor Boris Karloff in the 1935 horror classic Bride of Frankenstein. But it's clear the figure has seen better days and will need plenty of work to bring it back to life. Meanwhile, the curators discover photographs of the real Alice in Wonderland and repairs are made to Shakespeare's First Folio

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri travel to Georgian mansion of Tatton Park in Cheshire, where Bendor has spotted a mysterious portrait of a 16th-century physician in its collection

programme.title: Creating a Scene: BBC Introducing Arts
programme.desc: Chizzy Akudolu presents a collection of dramatic short films from new British directors, with subjects ranging from speed dating and school friendships, to spotting UFOs

programme.title: Hollywood's Brightest Bombshell: The Hedy Lamarr Story
programme.desc: The life and career of the actress, who fled from her Nazi-sympathiser husband in Austria and became a Hollywood star, and also co-invented 1940s wireless technology

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to create a deep forest with tall majestic trees, rain puddles and dark fresh air

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Made in Britain
programme.desc: Fred goes to Buckingham Palace to collect his MBE. Before his royal appointment, he makes a detour to the Crossness Pumping Station, in east London, where volunteers spent 18 years restoring four great beam engines, which are possibly the world's largest

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Bendor Grosvenor and Emma Dabiri travel to Georgian mansion of Tatton Park in Cheshire, where Bendor has spotted a mysterious portrait of a 16th-century physician in its collection

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Victoria and Albert curator Keith brings the original Frankenstein's monster out of the archives for a sci-fi exhibition, including the clothes worn by actor Boris Karloff in the 1935 horror classic Bride of Frankenstein. But it's clear the figure has seen better days and will need plenty of work to bring it back to life. Meanwhile, the curators discover photographs of the real Alice in Wonderland and repairs are made to Shakespeare's First Folio

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's World of Steam, Steel and Stone
programme.desc: The history of the steeplejack's favourite sites, beginning with his home town of Bolton, which was a hive of busy factories during the 1940s and 1950s when Britain led the industrialised world

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross creates a trail covered in fresh white snow, surrounded by warm colours and fuzzy bark trees, as he paints a brisk quiet day

programme.title: The Lake District: A Wild Year
programme.desc: For the wildlife and people who live amid the stunning scenery of the Lake District, life is one of continuous change. This documentary features cutting-edge camera techniques offering a new and unique perspective on a turbulent year in the life of England's largest national park. Time-lapse photography shows months and weeks passing in seconds - snow and ice giving way to sunshine or the frequent rain showers - while the animals, plants and people find extraordinary ways to cope with the challenges of this ancient landscape. Narrated by Bernard Cribbins

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Blencathra
programme.desc: Award-winning film-maker Terry Abraham returns to the Lake District to showcase 'the people's mountain' - Blencathra, looking at the lives of local residents, schoolchildren and tourists. Featuring contributions from comedian Ed Byrne, broadcaster Stuart Maconie, mountaineer Alan Hinkes OBE and record-breaking fell runner Steve Birkinshaw

programme.title: Britain's Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: An investigation into how recent archaeological finds on the archipelago indicate that Orkney was the cultural capital of Stone Age Britain and the birthplace of the stone circle cult. Chris Packham uncovers the secrets revealed by the DNA of Orkney's unique vole, Neil Oliver explores tombs and monuments, Shini Somara experiments to discover how ancient Orcadians could have moved giant blocks of stone over rough ground, and Andy Torbet climbs a challenging sea-stack to unlock the story of Orkney's unusual geology

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: Broadcaster David Dimbleby sails his yacht Rocket around the British coastline, exploring different aspects of the nation's maritime history, art and architecture along the way. He begins by sailing the south-west coast of Devon and Cornwall, where he tells the story of Sir Francis Drake and an array of adventurers, explorers, pirates and smugglers. He also welcomes record-breaking solo yachtswoman Ellen MacArthur on board and learns about one of the oldest maritime art forms of all - the tattoo, before agreeing to have one himself

programme.title: The Man Who Destroyed Everything
programme.desc: Profile of artist Michael Landy, examining his motives for destroying everything he owned during a two-week smashing spree in front of bemused onlookers in London's Oxford Street, and following his subsequent attempt to return to mainstream society. A repeat of the very first programme to be broadcast by BBC Four on its launch night in 2002

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts visits archaeological digs in the east of Britain. Finds include new revelations from a 3,000-year-old perfectly preserved village near Cambridgeshire, and the theatre where Shakespeare premiered Romeo and Juliet

programme.title: The Secret Life of Bob Monkhouse
programme.desc: The story of the comedian and game show host's life and career, told through the archive of films, TV shows, letters and memorabilia that he left behind after his death in 2003. Contributors include Ronnie Corbett, Barry Cryer, Michael Grade and Lenny Henry

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: The comedian takes over the science programme in a special episode for BBC's sitcom season, turning it into a chat show with eminent scientists as guests. He talks to them about the nature of laughter, with the studio audiences serving as guinea pigs in his attempt to get to the bottom of what laughter is, what it has to do with comedy, and why human beings find it a pleasurable sensation. The programme reveals that laughter predates the human species and may have played a vital role in our evolution

He talks about Claudia Jones, who founded the West Indian Gazette and inspired the Notting Hill Carnival, and also discusses Dr Harold Moody, who campaigned against racial injustice in the UK. He also shares his passion for helping his community and offers advice for how viewers can have a positive impact on their local areas.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse's over-reliance on gadgets prompts Colonel K to send his top agent on a tech-free nature retreat. Unbeknownst to Danger Mouse, Greenback has the same plans for his daughter, Delilah, whose social media obsession has also spiralled out of control. Back at HQ Penfold is tasked with protecting the entire world from the threat of super-villainy. Stripped off his gadgets, DM struggles to adapt to life in the wilderness and, when a talking pinecone called Conni begins to offer him survival tips, he begins to question his own sanity. Soon enough, Delilah catches wind of DM's presence and she plots to destroy him as a means of getting back into her Dad's good books. With the voices of Alexander Armstrong and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: The Deep

programme.title: Hank Zipzer
programme.desc: Hank is determined to make it through the last day of term without a detention - but an incident with an egg in assembly changes all of that and he finds himself in danger of being excluded from school permanently. Comedy, starring Nick James and Henry Winkler

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Documentary following 13-year-old Nigerian hyperrealism artist Kareem Waris as he stages his first exhibition in his hometown of Lagos. The film introduces Kareem and his family, before heading to the Ayowole Academy of Art, a makeshift art studio in a poor neighborhood in Lagos. From the picture he wants to draw of his grandma in a village many miles away, to the picture he wants to draw of Olu’s brother Samuel - a child amputee - through to the fishermen of the Makoko floating village and the elephants of Oyo National Park. These poignant pictures and their settings, hope to showcase the vibrancy, colour and beauty in Africa and evoke the wonder of Wakanda in Black Panther. After the pictures are created, it's on to the exhibition space, where many of Kareems peers and heroes await, in what promises to be an emotional and life-changing experience for Kareem and his family as he finally takes the first steps towards becoming a young master.

programme.desc: Denise's disappearance leaves her family and friends concerned, especially Chelsea, who is in a state of panic. programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The competition reaches the halfway point, where the contestants are challenged by Mary Berry to prepare the ultimate Sunday roast. The only rule is that the meal must involve roasted meat and some form of potatoes - they are otherwise free to do whatever they want. Two cooks compete to produce the best pork crackling, while another adds a Morrocan twist. For the second task, Chris Bavin challenges the cooks to make banana bread without using flour, while the final round involves a classic French dessert

programme.desc: The presenting skills of the contestants are tested as they host their own daytime magazine show Morning Glory, with Lorraine Kelly joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

Derry Girls actress Siobhan McSweeney idscusses becoming host of The Great Pottery Throw Down and comedian Alan Carr reflects on taking the helm as host of Interior Design Masters. Plus, singer and podcaster Jessie Ware performs her new single Remember Where You Are

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v West Ham United (Kick-off 7.30pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the tie at Old Trafford, as the teams contest a place in the last eight. United prevailed 3-2 here against rivals Liverpool in the previous round, with Bruno Fernandes coming off the bench to score the winner. The Hammers' progress was considerably more serene, as they recorded a 4-0 victory at home to League One side Doncaster Rovers. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Micah Richards

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian has never been very good at DIY, finding even the simple task of hanging a picture invariably ending in disaster. So it remains to be seen how he will cope after turning up at one of Wales's biggest building sites and being expected to get stuck in. From bricklaying to plumbing and carpentry, Rhod has to go all out to prove he has what it takes to become a master builder

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the pandemic's ongoing impact on performing arts in the UK. Featuring footage shot throughout 2020 documenting the ways people and organisations have strived to keep the industry alive, from ballet and opera to comedy clubs and live music venues. The film explores innovative new approaches to performance, from drive-in opera to world premiere dance pieces, as well as examining how the wider political backdrop of current events has shaped contemporary art. With contributions from Carlos Acosta, Tamara Rojo, James Graham, Matthew Warchus, Roderick Williams and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Musicals: The Greatest Show
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith introduces a celebration of the world of musical theatre from the London Palladium, with the host joining Amanda Holden to perform I Know Him So Well from Chess. There are also numbers from The Greatest Showman, Mamma Mia!, Hamilton, Hairspray, Funny Girl, The Phantom of the Opera and Dreamgirls, while international superstar Idina Menzel talks to West End legend Elaine Paige

programme.desc: In the quarter-final, the remaining celebrities show off their celebration cakes for Mary Berry's Ultimate Challenge. programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from six of the fifth-round matches, including Everton v Tottenham Hotspur, Swansea City v Manchester City and Manchester United v West Ham United. Manchester City have once again avoided Premier League opposition in their quest to reach the quarter-finals, leaving much of the attention of the matches at Goodison Park and Old Trafford, which featured four sides in the top half of the top flight. Plus, highlights of Leicester City v Brighton & Hove Albion, Sheffield United v Bristol City and Burnley v Bournemouth

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel are still in with a chance of making the play-offs, but their last few matches are critical as they face off against some of the toughest teams in the league. Can the players, coaches Joey and Fabian rise to the challenge and prove they deserve to be in the top six? Patrik opens up about his issues with weight, and reveals that his parents have never come to see him play League of Legends.

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ashes-winning captain Michael Vaughan, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, world champion speed-skater Elise Christie and Great Britain sprint great John Regis join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Denise's disappearance leaves her family and friends concerned, especially Chelsea, who is in a state of panic. Max babysits for Abi, causing him to reminisce about his much-missed daughter and how his life has not worked out the way he would have wanted. Dotty pushes Kush to decide whether he wants the spare room in her house, while the call centre has a new starter - Big Mo

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack welcome Sara Pascoe, Dan Walker, Nicola Coughlan and Mr Motivator to the comedy panel show on which the teams reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The competition reaches the halfway point, where the contestants are challenged by Mary Berry to prepare the ultimate Sunday roast. The only rule is that the meal must involve roasted meat and some form of potatoes - they are otherwise free to do whatever they want. Two cooks compete to produce the best pork crackling, while another adds a Morrocan twist. For the second task, Chris Bavin challenges the cooks to make banana bread without using flour, while the final round involves a classic French dessert

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: The presenting skills of the contestants are tested as they host their own daytime magazine show Morning Glory, with Lorraine Kelly joining Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by star of stage and screen Felicity Kendal, who is making her musical theatre debut in Anything Goes. Brit Regé-Jean Page, star of the hit Netflix drama Bridgerton, also appears as does rock legend Dave Grohl, who is promoting the new Foo Fighters album Medicine at Midnight. Derry Girls actress Siobhan McSweeney idscusses becoming host of The Great Pottery Throw Down and comedian Alan Carr reflects on taking the helm as host of Interior Design Masters. Plus, singer and podcaster Jessie Ware performs her new single Remember Where You Are

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Manchester United v West Ham United (Kick-off 7.30pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the tie at Old Trafford, as the teams contest a place in the last eight. United prevailed 3-2 here against rivals Liverpool in the previous round, with Bruno Fernandes coming off the bench to score the winner. The Hammers' progress was considerably more serene, as they recorded a 4-0 victory at home to League One side Doncaster Rovers. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Micah Richards

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes discovers that Peggy Piper, the Pernod poisoner, is being released from prison and could be coming back to town - which is bad news for Winnie as she was the one who saw her old friend poison her husband and gave the evidence that put her behind bars in the first place. Meanwhile, Dermot hires an expensive tank costume for his latest promotion - only for Buster to go and lose it. Starring Brendan O'Carroll, Dermot O'Neill, Jennifer Gibney, Gary Hollywood and guest starring Sue Vincent

programme.title: Imagine
programme.desc: Alan Yentob explores the pandemic's ongoing impact on performing arts in the UK. Featuring footage shot throughout 2020 documenting the ways people and organisations have strived to keep the industry alive, from ballet and opera to comedy clubs and live music venues. The film explores innovative new approaches to performance, from drive-in opera to world premiere dance pieces, as well as examining how the wider political backdrop of current events has shaped contemporary art. With contributions from Carlos Acosta, Tamara Rojo, James Graham, Matthew Warchus, Roderick Williams and Vanessa Redgrave

programme.title: Musicals: The Greatest Show
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith introduces a celebration of the world of musical theatre from the London Palladium, with the host joining Amanda Holden to perform I Know Him So Well from Chess. There are also numbers from The Greatest Showman, Mamma Mia!, Hamilton, Hairspray, Funny Girl, The Phantom of the Opera and Dreamgirls, while international superstar Idina Menzel talks to West End legend Elaine Paige

programme.desc: Five dealers bid against one another to buy a collection of curiosities, an unusual chair, a mask, candlesticks from a famous maker, and a writing slope. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio, presented by Alex Jones and Ronan Keating

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a clock made from the propeller of a First World War plane, a pair of stained glass windows, and an ancient shoe-stretching machine

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max reaches out to an unlikely source, causing him to break down, clearly unable to cope. Kathy feels lost with Ian still missing, so Bobby suggests she sees a doctor. A desperate Shirley and Mick learn that a woman matching Tina's description has been spotted and decide to take matters into their own hands

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha gets the shock of his life when a dangerous and vulnerable Jodie appears back on the scene. Kian's new boss Sahira breezes back into Holby, much to everyone's surprise. An outgoing new nurse has her first day working in a busy AAU, which proves to be a trial by fire

programme.title: Celebrity Best Home Cook
programme.desc: In the quarter-final, the remaining celebrities show off their celebration cakes for Mary Berry's Ultimate Challenge. They also take part in Chris Bavin's all-vegetarian Rustle Up, tasked with making the humble chickpea the star of their dishes, in a bid to avoid Angela Hartnett's dreaded Eliminator. Presented by Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Nolan Live
programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates, with viewers sending in their questions via text, email and tweet

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from six of the fifth-round matches, including Everton v Tottenham Hotspur, Swansea City v Manchester City and Manchester United v West Ham United. Manchester City have once again avoided Premier League opposition in their quest to reach the quarter-finals, leaving much of the attention of the matches at Goodison Park and Old Trafford, which featured four sides in the top half of the top flight. Plus, highlights of Leicester City v Brighton & Hove Albion, Sheffield United v Bristol City and Burnley v Bournemouth

programme.title: Fight for First: Excel Esports
programme.desc: Excel are still in with a chance of making the play-offs, but their last few matches are critical as they face off against some of the toughest teams in the league. Can the players, coaches Joey and Fabian rise to the challenge and prove they deserve to be in the top six? Patrik opens up about his issues with weight, and reveals that his parents have never come to see him play League of Legends.

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Ashes-winning captain Michael Vaughan, Olympic silver medal-winning swimmer Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, world champion speed-skater Elise Christie and Great Britain sprint great John Regis join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint Lords and Commons committee session examining the UK government's review of the Human Rights Act 1998, with evidence from the former president of the UK supreme court Baroness Hale, from Wednesday 3 February.

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Defence Committee session on NATO, US and UK defence relations, with evidence from defence and diplomatic experts, from Tuesday 2 February

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Acts and Facts
programme.desc: Historian Sean Lang delivers a short lecture on Warren Hastings who was tried for impeachment over what was claimed to be his corrupt government of India

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Work and Pensions Committee session on the UK government department's response to the Covid-19 pandemic, with evidence from the work and pensions secretary Thérèse Coffey, from Wednesday 3 February.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: The introduction to the House of Commons of a Bill by Conservative MP Paul Maynard on reviewing local welfare assistance, from earlier today.

programme.title: Acts and Facts
programme.desc: Historian Kate Williams delivers a short lecture on the Introduction of compulsory military service in 1916

programme.title: Business Questions

programme.desc: Matilda wakes up and discovers there is a puppet on her bed. When she puts it on her hand, it comes to life and is able to move and talk

programme.title: DIY le Donnie

The DIY expert also visits a manse renovation project in Point, Isle of Lewis

programme.title: Sgeul Slainte

programme.title: TradFest
programme.desc: A selection of some of the best music from Tradfest Music Festival in Dublin, presented by Allan MacDonald. Showcasing brilliant musicians from Irish and traditional music, TradFest brings us a range of the best talent there is to find, with some of the biggest names in trad and Irish music. Amongst the performers are Eoghan Ó Ceannabháin, An teaghlach tàlantach, Mick, Aoife & Ciara O'Brien and Blazin' Fiddles

programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts as Elvira and her son Emmet from Bedford, London couple Charlie and Paul, and Matt and his son Eddie from Wiltshire use their powers of deduction to try to work out words that appear in the Lingo grids and Puzzlewords

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Claudia, Neville, Kim and Pete pit their wits against quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but if anyone is caught they're out for good, leaving fewer players for the final chase

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gabby shows potential to Kim, Noah is annoyed by Charity's interference, and Matty and Amy arrange a date

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Leanne lies to Nick, making out that Imran's keeping her job open for her and she is fine for money. Having failed to secure a mortgage, David and Shona are at their wit's end. Johnny is horrified when he fails to recognise a cry for help from Joe, a suicidal fellow prisoner

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The duo continue their European adventure in Italy, starting in the picturesque Lake Garda, where Barney surprises his dad by explaining they'll be running off the top of Monte Baldo and paragliding over the lake below. The next day Bradley is treated to a hair-raising ride in a Formula 1 speed boat, before the Walshes are invited to the home of opera superstar Andrea Bocelli

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David and Tracy start a frenzied bidding war over Number 8, while Jenny is worried sick, aware that Joe's overdose will bring back dark thoughts of Aidan for Johnny. Debbie reveals to Ray's accountant, Miles, how she's no intention of allowing Crosby a new life in Turkey

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell help more people to reunite with long-lost relatives. Claire Martin was abandoned in Hong Kong as a baby more than 50 years ago and heads back there to appeal for information about her origins, but with the help of DNA she finds family much closer to home. The team helps Nicki Goscomb and her sisters search for their brother, who was given up for adoption in 1966

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Home Sweet Home - Travels in My Own Land
programme.desc: Starting in the Outer Hebrides, Joanna explores the Harris Tweed industry, and drives a herd of Highland Cattle across the sands at low tide to their winter grazing. Next she heads to Eilean Donan Castle, the location for her first episode of The New Avengers. Crossing to Northern Ireland, Joanna visits the Dark Hedges, made famous in Game of Thrones, before having a laugh with one of the Derry Girls. She then meets the inspiring founder of the Children in Crossfire charity

programme.title: Marcella
programme.desc: Marcella's mental state is beginning to fracture. Bobby is arrested and Katherine takes control of the city by getting rid of those in her way. Crime drama, starring Anna Friel

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams exposes TV's biggest clangers, gaffes and fluffed cues, all rescued from the cutting-room floor. This time, Brenda Blethyn has a run-in with an amorous puppy, a Downing Street reporter swallows a fly and Stephen Mulhern faces a potty-mouthed member of the public. There are also mistakes featuring Martin Clunes, Ant & Dec, Will.i.am, Amanda Holden, Paddy McGuiness, and the stars of Love Island

programme.title: The Bay
programme.desc: As the team press on with the investigation, Lisa and Med dig deeper into the family and discover not all relationships with the victim were amicable. Starring Morven Christie and Daniel Ryan

programme.desc: Mavis agrees to sell her house to Fred. Steve is taken in for questioning on a drugs charge. Des and Samantha go off on holiday

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
