programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff asks Tara to call off the wedding. Vic gets jealous when Viv and Terry dance together at Will's party. Steve causes a tragic accident

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy is rushed to hospital with a brain contusion. Tara assures Biff he will not lose her. Kim is racked with guilt and blames herself for Kathy's accident

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Carol is smitten with handsome visitor Major Giles MacLean, but Dawson, Wetherby and Mason suspect he could be involved in a spate of burglaries targeting the wealthiest homes in the district. Meanwhile, Peggy sets David up with an old friend of hers, a former circus performer, and Bernie worries when Rosie fails to return from Australia. Lisa Kay, Clare Wille, Rupert Ward-Lewis and Joe McFadden star

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers where the money for his birthday party came from. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James as the head of the family, struggling to keep his wife and children in order

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George has already denounced the neighbours as toffee-nosed snobs, and events make Mildred wonder whether he could be right. Queenie Watts and Michael Robbins guest star, with Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan as the genteel sleuth. A woman experiences an unsettling vision after moving into the home of her dreams - and it transpires she lived in the house as a child. Miss Marple investigates the history of the property and discovers it was connected with a baffling disappearance years before - which has never been solved. With Dawn French, Russ Abbot, Geraldine Chaplin, Phil Davis and Sarah Parish

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson try to trace Rosie's whereabouts in Australia, and Mick becomes a suspect when they uncover evidence of domestic violence in his relationship with her. Meanwhile in Brisbane, Carol visits a monastery to find out more about her long-lost brother, but her search leads to a trap when she receives a call from someone claiming to have information. Starring Joe McFadden, Clare Wille and Lisa Kay

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff is shocked when he hears about Kathy's accident. Terry flirts with Tricia in the Woolpack. Steve suggests to Kim that they avoid the pressure and make a run for it

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is annoyed when Kim decides to stay in the village. He goes to dig up the money but is interrupted by Chris. Zak and Butch plan to burgle the garden centre

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud fears she is going to lose Maureen to Fred when they get married. Steve plans to move away from Weatherfield. Jim tells Fiona there is a chance he is the father of her baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona is sickened at the thought of carrying Jim's baby. Kevin talks Natalie out of reporting Sally to the police. Percy is puzzled to find things missing in Emily's house

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A prostitute's client confesses to the murder of another working girl before vanishing into the night, leaving Wycliffe with the tricky task of tracking him down before he kills again. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the mysterious death of businessman John Shearwood, found floating in the water under the Gateshead Millennium Bridge when he was supposed to be on a golfing trip to Ireland. The man's wife Stella and brother Luke are baffled to discover he was working as an informant for HM Revenue and Customs. Chief Revenue investigator Owen Preece (guest star Robert Glenister) reveals John's business manager Mark Donovan has been running an alcohol scam through their haulage firm and the deceased had agreed to offer information in exchange for immunity from prosecution. Was his cover blown? Owen doesn't think so, and Mark has an alibi - unlike John's wife. Brenda Blethyn stars, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Racial tensions erupt in Hastings when a black GI is suspected of murdering a local woman. The victim, an ex-lover of the suspect and the mother of his baby, was staying at a guest house where Samantha is assistant manager, and had been disowned by her family because of her mixed-race child. As Foyle investigates, he is not convinced the soldier could be capable of such a crime, and soon comes up against the might of the US Army as he tries to crack the case. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Obi Abili and Charlotte Riley

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The apparent accidental death of a drug addict takes a surprising turn when the results of the post-mortem examination are revealed, and Jack finds himself confronting his own chequered past. Ewen Bremner guest-stars, with David Jason

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson continue their search for Rosie in Australia, and after ruling out Mick as a suspect, turn their attention to a local roadhouse near to where a missing woman's car was found abandoned. The search is on for the bar owner's son when the police discover he has a criminal record. Meanwhile, Carol delivers Nev's sister's baby and is later overjoyed when long-lost brother Danny arrives in town. Starring Joe McFadden and Clare Wille

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is planning the robbery when the police arrive to search his house. Steve plans to flee to Venezuela. Gossip about the Dingles spreads around the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles are angry that people think they are guilty. Kim implores Steve to cancel the flights. Rachel lambasts Chris for never spending any time with Joseph

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin is furious to hear Natalie reported Sally to the police. Fiona feels trapped in a hopeless situation. Martin is surprised to find Don in hospital under guard

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie gives Sally an ultimatum. Emily worries that Percy is acting strangely. Martin persuades Gail to visit Don in hospital. Maud tells Maureen she is moving into Mayfield Court

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate crime and mayhem in the countryside as they take on a murder case in picturesque Fletcher's Cross. Undertaker Patrick Pennyman is found dead in his funeral parlour and the detectives suspect a possible link to the local spiritualist church. When another body turns up, the pressure to find the culprit redoubles. John Nettles, John Hopkins and Jane Wymark star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A climber plunges to his death from a cliff face and a police informant claims it was no accident. A re-enactment of the incident adds fuel to the fire, and Wycliffe braces himself to interview the dead man's best friend. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Lynn Farleigh, Christian Solimeno and Paul Venables

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby decides to join Causton's annual trip to Brighton when he becomes suspicious about the mayor's plans to buy up coastal land for holiday chalets. But before he can get to the bottom of the dubious deal, a hated property developer is beheaded at a funfair and a collection of valuable historical swords goes missing. With the help of his cousin DCI John Barnaby, the detective sets out to crack the case. Drama, guest starring Neil Dudgeon (Roman's Empire), Mark Gatiss (The League of Gentlemen), Coronation Street villain Brian Capron and Blackadder's Tim McInnerny

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The surly detective tries to get out of attending a reunion of George Cross holders because a serial rapist has struck again on his patch. Righteous indignation prompts him to take the most desperate professional gamble of his entire career by using PC Hazel Wallace as bait in a trap. He eventually picks up a lead involving the most recent victim's father, but comes under pressure from within the force to back down or be thrown off the case. David Jason stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: No-nonsense teacher Jim Osgood uses his experience of the Territorial Army to impose strict discipline at Ashfordly School, but he goes too far with schoolboy troublemaker Gary Bell and puts him in hospital. Determined to get revenge, the youngster breaks into Osgood's office and steals his gun, leaving Mason with little time to avert a tragedy. Joe McFadden and Lisa Kay star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is upset when Kim refuses to tell him where the money is. Terry takes Tricia clothes shopping. Paddy's date with Jane goes surprisingly well

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris presses Zoe for more information about the night of the robbery. Alan warns Tricia about Terry. Zak vows to fight the eviction from his home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley is shocked to hear that Don is dying, and Alma builds up her courage to face Brennan. Kevin tells Sally that Natalie will drop the charges if she apologises

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Having seen Don, Alma feels positive about the future, but Martin and Gail later hear Brennan is on the loose. Natalie tells Sally she overreacted and will not take her to court

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when ladies' man and rowing club member Guy Sweetman is found dead in the river during the annual Midsomer Regatta. They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A policeman's murder gives Wycliffe plenty to think about - especially as the chief suspect is a fellow officer who has been having an affair with the victim's wife. Adding to the pressure, senior figures within the force want the matter cleared up as quickly as possible to avoid a scandal. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans steps into the shoes of John Thaw in this 1965-set prequel to Inspector Morse, playing a younger version of the cerebral detective involved in the hunt for a missing schoolgirl. Soon deep in a full-blown murder investigation, Endeavour is sidelined, discredited and at a dead end - so he risks all to begin his own quest for justice. Roger Allam and Charlie Creed-Miles co-star in the mystery

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It's 1946 and although one war has ended, another is looming as the Iron Curtain falls across Europe. Foyle arrives home from America, where he is wanted in connection with a suspicious suicide, but MI5 offers him a way out. The agency suspects Britain's atomic research programme has been infiltrated and wants to recruit him to investigate a Soviet spy ring that could be at work in the heart of London. Detective drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Honeysuckle Weeks, Ellie Haddington and Dylan Charles

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack tackles what promises to be the most sensitive and unenviable investigation of his entire career - deciding whether a man with Down's syndrome is capable of committing murder. David Jason stars as the caustic copper, with Bruce Alexander and Matthew Marsh

Meanwhile in Brisbane, Carol visits a monastery to find out more about her long-lost brother, but her search leads to a trap when she receives a call from someone claiming to have information. Starring Joe McFadden, Clare Wille and Lisa Kay

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff is shocked when he hears about Kathy's accident. Terry flirts with Tricia in the Woolpack. Steve suggests to Kim that they avoid the pressure and make a run for it

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is annoyed when Kim decides to stay in the village. He goes to dig up the money but is interrupted by Chris. Zak and Butch plan to burgle the garden centre

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud fears she is going to lose Maureen to Fred when they get married. Steve plans to move away from Weatherfield. Jim tells Fiona there is a chance he is the father of her baby

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona is sickened at the thought of carrying Jim's baby. Kevin talks Natalie out of reporting Sally to the police. Percy is puzzled to find things missing in Emily's house

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A prostitute's client confesses to the murder of another working girl before vanishing into the night, leaving Wycliffe with the tricky task of tracking him down before he kills again. Detective drama, starring Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the mysterious death of businessman John Shearwood, found floating in the water under the Gateshead Millennium Bridge when he was supposed to be on a golfing trip to Ireland. The man's wife Stella and brother Luke are baffled to discover he was working as an informant for HM Revenue and Customs. Chief Revenue investigator Owen Preece (guest star Robert Glenister) reveals John's business manager Mark Donovan has been running an alcohol scam through their haulage firm and the deceased had agreed to offer information in exchange for immunity from prosecution. Was his cover blown? Owen doesn't think so, and Mark has an alibi - unlike John's wife. Brenda Blethyn stars, with David Leon as her trusty sidekick Joe

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: Racial tensions erupt in Hastings when a black GI is suspected of murdering a local woman. The victim, an ex-lover of the suspect and the mother of his baby, was staying at a guest house where Samantha is assistant manager, and had been disowned by her family because of her mixed-race child. As Foyle investigates, he is not convinced the soldier could be capable of such a crime, and soon comes up against the might of the US Army as he tries to crack the case. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Obi Abili and Charlotte Riley

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The apparent accidental death of a drug addict takes a surprising turn when the results of the post-mortem examination are revealed, and Jack finds himself confronting his own chequered past. Ewen Bremner guest-stars, with David Jason

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid discovers why Jean is sulking and Sally and Mike will not talk to him. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's jealousy rears its ugly head, but Mildred is in no hurry to put his mind at rest

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster teams up with a dashing man to investigate a series of poison-pen letters in the sleepy village of Lymstock. Two of the missives have led to the deaths of Mona Symmington, the wife of Lymstock solicitor Richard, and Agnes Woddell, the parlour maid at the Symmingtons' house. However, Miss Marple suspects there is more to these deaths than meets the eye. Murder mystery with an all-star cast, featuring Geraldine McEwan, James D'Arcy, Emilia Fox, Imogen Stubbs, Ken Russell, Harry Enfield and Kelly Brook

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Mason and Dawson continue their search for Rosie in Australia, and after ruling out Mick as a suspect, turn their attention to a local roadhouse near to where a missing woman's car was found abandoned. The search is on for the bar owner's son when the police discover he has a criminal record. Meanwhile, Carol delivers Nev's sister's baby and is later overjoyed when long-lost brother Danny arrives in town. Starring Joe McFadden and Clare Wille

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is planning the robbery when the police arrive to search his house. Steve plans to flee to Venezuela. Gossip about the Dingles spreads around the village

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Dingles are angry that people think they are guilty. Kim implores Steve to cancel the flights. Rachel lambasts Chris for never spending any time with Joseph

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin is furious to hear Natalie reported Sally to the police. Fiona feels trapped in a hopeless situation. Martin is surprised to find Don in hospital under guard

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie gives Sally an ultimatum. Emily worries that Percy is acting strangely. Martin persuades Gail to visit Don in hospital. Maud tells Maureen she is moving into Mayfield Court

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate crime and mayhem in the countryside as they take on a murder case in picturesque Fletcher's Cross. Undertaker Patrick Pennyman is found dead in his funeral parlour and the detectives suspect a possible link to the local spiritualist church. When another body turns up, the pressure to find the culprit redoubles. John Nettles, John Hopkins and Jane Wymark star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A climber plunges to his death from a cliff face and a police informant claims it was no accident. A re-enactment of the incident adds fuel to the fire, and Wycliffe braces himself to interview the dead man's best friend. Drama, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters, Lynn Farleigh, Christian Solimeno and Paul Venables

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby decides to join Causton's annual trip to Brighton when he becomes suspicious about the mayor's plans to buy up coastal land for holiday chalets. But before he can get to the bottom of the dubious deal, a hated property developer is beheaded at a funfair and a collection of valuable historical swords goes missing. With the help of his cousin DCI John Barnaby, the detective sets out to crack the case. Drama, guest starring Neil Dudgeon (Roman's Empire), Mark Gatiss (The League of Gentlemen), Coronation Street villain Brian Capron and Blackadder's Tim McInnerny

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: As Foyle prepares to be replaced at the police station and begin retirement, his attention is grabbed by a news story about a man sentenced to hang for high treason, and he decides to take on the case. But with the prisoner's execution date approaching, the detective has to work fast to find evidence that will clear him. Meanwhile, Samantha and Adam fight to save Hill House from the town planners determined to demolish it - but disaster soon strikes. Starring Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks and Max Brown

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The surly detective tries to get out of attending a reunion of George Cross holders because a serial rapist has struck again on his patch. Righteous indignation prompts him to take the most desperate professional gamble of his entire career by using PC Hazel Wallace as bait in a trap. He eventually picks up a lead involving the most recent victim's father, but comes under pressure from within the force to back down or be thrown off the case. David Jason stars

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George's brother discovers documents that prove Mildred married an Army deserter. Comedy, starring Yootha Joyce and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuthing spinster meets fellow enthusiasts Tommy and Tuppence at a nursing home, where the ramblings of an elderly resident prompt them to join forces and investigate the unsolved mystery of a murdered child. The trail leads to the Norfolk village of Farrell St Edmund, a community harbouring sinister secrets and tales of heartbreak. Geraldine McEwan heads an all-star cast, including Greta Scacchi, Anthony Andrews, Charles Dance, June Whitfield and Claire Bloom

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: No-nonsense teacher Jim Osgood uses his experience of the Territorial Army to impose strict discipline at Ashfordly School, but he goes too far with schoolboy troublemaker Gary Bell and puts him in hospital. Determined to get revenge, the youngster breaks into Osgood's office and steals his gun, leaving Mason with little time to avert a tragedy. Joe McFadden and Lisa Kay star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve is upset when Kim refuses to tell him where the money is. Terry takes Tricia clothes shopping. Paddy's date with Jane goes surprisingly well

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris presses Zoe for more information about the night of the robbery. Alan warns Tricia about Terry. Zak vows to fight the eviction from his home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley is shocked to hear that Don is dying, and Alma builds up her courage to face Brennan. Kevin tells Sally that Natalie will drop the charges if she apologises

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Having seen Don, Alma feels positive about the future, but Martin and Gail later hear Brennan is on the loose. Natalie tells Sally she overreacted and will not take her to court

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate when ladies' man and rowing club member Guy Sweetman is found dead in the river during the annual Midsomer Regatta. They soon discover the elite exterior of the club hides a world of money problems and sexual jealousy and the victim had a number of enemies who might have wanted him dead. Guest starring Olympic rower Steve Redgrave and Diana Quick

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: A policeman's murder gives Wycliffe plenty to think about - especially as the chief suspect is a fellow officer who has been having an affair with the victim's wife. Adding to the pressure, senior figures within the force want the matter cleared up as quickly as possible to avoid a scandal. Jack Shepherd and Helen Masters star

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Shaun Evans steps into the shoes of John Thaw in this 1965-set prequel to Inspector Morse, playing a younger version of the cerebral detective involved in the hunt for a missing schoolgirl. Soon deep in a full-blown murder investigation, Endeavour is sidelined, discredited and at a dead end - so he risks all to begin his own quest for justice. Roger Allam and Charlie Creed-Miles co-star in the mystery

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It's 1946 and although one war has ended, another is looming as the Iron Curtain falls across Europe. Foyle arrives home from America, where he is wanted in connection with a suspicious suicide, but MI5 offers him a way out. The agency suspects Britain's atomic research programme has been infiltrated and wants to recruit him to investigate a Soviet spy ring that could be at work in the heart of London. Detective drama, starring Michael Kitchen, with Honeysuckle Weeks, Ellie Haddington and Dylan Charles

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack tackles what promises to be the most sensitive and unenviable investigation of his entire career - deciding whether a man with Down's syndrome is capable of committing murder. David Jason stars as the caustic copper, with Bruce Alexander and Matthew Marsh

programme.desc: The duo try to track down an American colonel tormented by memories his harrowing experiences while in command of an air base in Vietnam. Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter star

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: An interpreter claims to have been abducted by two villains who forced him to interrogate a badly bruised and obviously distressed Greek captive unable to speak English. Period detective drama, starring Jeremy Brett

programme.title: Dempsey and Makepeace
programme.desc: Dempsey comes up against a man who is his equal in all respects. The only difference is that he's on the wrong side of the law. Obsessed with capturing him, Dempsey tests Makepeace's patience to the limit. Will Dempsey succeed against impossible odds?

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The university branch of a bank is robbed, and though Regan and the Flying Squad catch most of the gang a known killer and his sidekick remain inside holding the manager and two women hostage. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and Mrs Peel investigate reports of a traitor in the ranks, and end up facing the heart-breaking prospect of bidding each other farewell. Patrick Macnee stars, with Diana Rigg making her final appearance as Emma and Linda Thorson taking over as Steed's latest partner Tara King

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur spies a business opportunity as he and Ray prepare to pay their respects to the late Charlie Johnson, but the deceased's widow has other ideas. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: The Motorbike Show
programme.desc: Henry Cole discovers the joys of Portugal's roads on a visit to the Algarve and joins Sam Lovegrove on the epic Pioneer Run, racing from London to Brighton on pre-1915 bikes

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A solicitor enlists Holmes to help clear him of murdering eccentric builder Jonas Oldacre, whose considerable personal fortune he stood to inherit. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett as the famed Victorian detective

programme.desc: Tara and Steed try and put all the pieces together when people start turning up dead in unique locations with a jigsaw puzzle piece placed on their persons. Peter Jeffrey guest stars

programme.title: Extreme Salvage Squad
programme.desc: The concrete boat job has turned into a nightmare. The houseboat has collapsed in on itself and there's mess and concrete debris everywhere. While Luke, Jayde and Ellie navigate the extreme humidity, mud and mangroves, Dan and Jordan are off to evaluate their next job. The Belle Canto is smashed upon the rocks. It's a total write-off and they wont be able to use Christopher to winch it out. To make matters worse, Jordan has lost control of the team's dingy.

programme.title: Jaws (1975)
programme.desc: A New England seaside town's tourist season is turned into a nightmare by a giant killer shark. The local police chief's efforts to protect the public are thwarted by the greedy mayor, forcing him to join forces with a grizzled fisherman and an enthusiastic marine biologist to hunt and kill the monster predator. Steven Spielberg's thriller, starring Roy Scheider, Richard Dreyfuss and Robert Shaw

programme.desc: The William Norling is seven days into a nine-day run and Skipper Danny is no closer to finding a buyer for his 1,000kg of lobster. He's running up huge operating costs and desperately needs a sale to stay afloat.

programme.title: RoboCop 2 (1990)
programme.desc: The cybernetic law enforcer returns to battle a deranged drug lord. Meanwhile, he is unaware the corporation that created him has scheduled his replacement by a more powerful version. Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Peter Weller, Nancy Allen, Tom Noonan and Belinda Bauer

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The island of Andros in the West Indies boasts tales of swimmers being ripped from the surface of the island's mysterious blue holes by a monster known as the Lusca. Described as half-shark, half-octopus with a taste for human flesh, it is said to drag its prey a thousand feet down to its deep water lair. programme.title: Inside Iceland: Britain's Budget Supermarket
programme.desc: A current area for growth is vegan food, so Iceland's vegan buyer visits a factory to watch meatless sausage rolls being produced, as well as scoping out vegan products for the future. The Iceland team also tucks into its rivals' choc ices, while out in Italy the team develops a new ice-cream cake. Elsewhere, some environmentally friendly packaging for fruit and vegetables is put to the test with a view to making the company plastic free by 2023

programme.title: Secrets of Your Supermarket Food
programme.desc: Sian Williams, Stefan Gates and Nisha Katona reveal the truth about the fruit and vegetables bought in British supermarkets. With the help of dietitian Aisling Pigott, Sian discovers what actually counts as one of the recommended five a day, while Stefan goes behind the scenes of one of the largest soft fruit farms in the UK. Later, Nisha heads to Lancashire to discovers how mushrooms are grown and harvested

programme.title: The Disappearance of Shannon Matthews
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary about the disappearance of nine-year-old Shannon Matthews in Yorkshire in 2008, an investigation that gripped the nation, which turned out to be hoax kidnapping plot devised by her mother. Featuring exclusive interviews with Shannon's best friend, members of the local community, and the police officers who eventually found her, the programme reveals the remarkable truth of what really happened

programme.title: Words of Caramel
programme.desc: In a refugee camp in the Sahara desert lives a Deaf boy who wants to learn to write, to express the words of his camel friend

programme.title: Death Drums Along the River (1963)
programme.desc: A British inspector is called to Africa to investigate the murder of a fellow policeman and uncovers a diamond-smuggling operation seemingly connected to an upriver hospital. Mystery, based on Edgar Wallace's novel Sanders on the River, starring Richard Todd, Marianne Koch, Albert Lieven and Walter Rilla

programme.title: The Far Country (1954)
programme.desc: A cowpoke is steering his cattle north to the Canadian border when a corrupt sheriff commandeers the herd. A local saloon-bar owner falls for the hapless cowboy, and with her help he recaptures his cattle. Hooking up with a wagon train, the couple head for the mining town of Dawson, but the thwarted and embittered sheriff is on their tail. Western, with James Stewart, Walter Brennan and Ruth Roman

programme.title: When Eight Bells Toll (1971)
programme.desc: A Navy intelligence agent heads to Scotland, where he poses as a marine biologist to investigate the theft of millions of pounds in gold bullion from ships off the British coast. However, the locals counter his enquiries with hostility, especially when he sets his sights on a Greek tycoon, whom he suspects may be operating as a modern-day pirate. Thriller, with Anthony Hopkins, Robert Morley and Jack Hawkins. Based on a novel by Alistair MacLean

programme.title: This Happy Breed (1944)
programme.desc: A working-class family moves into a new suburban home after the father returns from service in the First World War. However, the couple and their children struggle to adjust to life higher up the social ladder. David Lean's drama, adapted by Noel Coward from his play, starring Robert Newton, Celia Johnson, Robert Stanley Holloway and John Mills

programme.title: The Imitation Game (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of mathematician Alan Turing, whose work during the Second World War translated seemingly unbreakable Nazi codes as well as pioneering the development of computer technology. In the years following the war, his life would be destroyed as his homosexuality was discovered, leaving him facing criminal charges. Starring Benedict Cumberbatch, Keira Knightley and Charles Dance

programme.title: Three Billboards Outside Ebbing, Missouri (2017)
programme.desc: Grieving mother Mildred Hayes has suffered an unimaginable personal loss, and hatches an idea that drags the residents of Ebbing, Missouri into a maelstrom of revenge. She pressures Sheriff Bill Willoughby and his forces into continuing the investigation into her daughter's murder. It becomes an obsession that has dramatic repercussions for all of the locals. Martin McDonagh's Bafta and Oscar-winning crime drama, starring Frances McDormand, Sam Rockwell and Woody Harrelson

programme.title: Double Jeopardy (1999)
programme.desc: A man fakes his own death and frames his wife for murder, but after six years in prison she is released. Since the woman has already served her time, she has nothing to lose by really killing him - but her mission of vengeance is complicated by a zealous parole officer determined to stop her. Thriller, starring Ashley Judd, Tommy Lee Jones, Bruce Greenwood and Annabeth Gish

programme.title: White God (2014)
programme.desc: Teenager Lili is devastated when her father chooses to abandon her beloved dog Hagen on the streets, rather than pay a new tax on non-pedigree canines. She sets out to find her pet, while Hagen embarks on an adventure of his own that sees him joining forces with a pack of fellow crossbreeds. Drama, starring Zsofia Psotta and Sandor Zsoter. In Hungarian and English

programme.title: My Man Godfrey (1936)
programme.desc: A spoiled aristocratic woman hires a tramp as her butler, little anticipating how valuable he will prove _ or what effect his charms will have on her. Classic comedy, starring William Powell, Carole Lombard and Alice Brady

programme.title: Heaven Knows, Mr Allison (1957)
programme.desc: A US marine corporal stationed on a South Pacific island and a novice nun on a mission are forced into the hills when Japanese forces arrive and set up military bases during the Second World War. Before long the pair develop strong emotional ties, but in resisting their feelings for each other the relationship becomes one of deep mutual respect. Romantic drama, written and directed by John Huston, with Robert Mitchum and Deborah Kerr

programme.title: The Guns of Fort Petticoat (1957)
programme.desc: A lieutenant deserts after his commanding officer launches an unprovoked attack on a Comanche village during the Civil War. Realising that there are no soldiers in his home town to protect it from reprisals, he returns to train the local women to defend themselves. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Kathryn Grant, Hope Emerson and Jeff Donnell

programme.title: Winchester '73 (1950)
programme.desc: A gunslinger tracks down his father's killer, but is unable to exact his revenge because the sheriff of the town makes a point of confiscating all firearms. Instead he challenges him to compete in a shooting competition to win a superb rifle, but when the murderer loses, he steals the coveted prize. Western, starring James Stewart, Shelley Winters, Dan Duryea and Stephen McNally

programme.title: X-Men: Days of Future Past (2014)
programme.desc: In a future ravaged by war, the superhero team makes a desperate last stand against a force of deadly robots. Their only hope is to send Wolverine back in time to the 1970s, to join forces with an earlier version of the X-Men and change the events that started the conflict. Superhero adventure sequel uniting the casts of the original movie and prequel First Class, starring Hugh Jackman, Jennifer Lawrence, James McAvoy and Michael Fassbender

programme.title: The Post (2017)
programme.desc: In 1971, an American military analyst uncovers the depths of the US government's deceptions about the futility of the Vietnam War, and takes action by copying top-secret documents that would become the Pentagon Papers. Washington Post owner Kay Graham and her editor Ben Bradlee discover The New York Times is preparing an explosive expose on those papers and, determined to compete, set out to publish them first. Steven Spielberg's fact-based drama, starring Meryl Streep, Tom Hanks, Sarah Paulson and Bob Odenkirk

programme.title: Snowden (2016)
programme.desc: Oliver Stone's fact-based thriller about US whistleblower Edward Snowden, who was working for America's National Security Agency when he made public thousands of classified documents. Joseph Gordon-Levitt stars, with Shailene Woodley, Melissa Leo, Zachary Quinto, Tom Wilkinson, Rhys Ifans and Nicolas Cage

programme.title: Black Mother (2018)
programme.desc: Documentary, directed by Khalik Allah, offering a deeply immersive and personal tribute to the contrasting aspects of Jamaica, its people and its history

programme.title: Bugles in the Afternoon (1952)
programme.desc: A disgraced cavalryman tries to redeem himself by returning to active service at a fort in the middle of Sioux territory, unaware the commanding officer is an arch-enemy from the past. Antagonism grows as they both fall for the same woman, and the newcomer finds himself assigned to ever more dangerous missions by his commander, who hopes he will fail to return from them. Western, with Ray Milland, Helena Carter and Hugh Marlowe

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen (1960)
programme.desc: A former Army colonel, angry at his enforced retirement, sets out to exact an elaborate revenge on society in general. Recruiting seven other former servicemen who have turned to a life of crime, he plans a major bank robbery with military precision. Crime caper, starring Jack Hawkins, Nigel Patrick, Richard Attenborough, Roger Livesey and Bryan Forbes

programme.title: The Black Tent (1956)
programme.desc: A wounded British Army captain is saved by a tribe of nomads in the Libyan desert during the Second World War, and they take him to their camp, where he is cared for by the sheikh's daughter and falls in love with her. Romantic drama, with Anthony Steel, Anna-Maria Sandri, Donald Sinden, Andre Morell and Donald Pleasence

programme.title: OSS (1946)
programme.desc: Alan Ladd is on fine form as a tough spy sent to France to collect vital information shortly before D-Day. His team of expert operatives includes a strong-minded female agent - but sparks fly when the pair begin to work together, a situation which looks likely to lead to a crisis of conscience. By-the-numbers World War Two adventure, with Geraldine Fitzgerald, Patric Knowles and Richard Benedict

programme.title: Home Again (2017)
programme.desc: Five months after separating from her husband, Alice Kinney and her daughters relocate from New York to her childhood home in Los Angeles, which belonged to her late father, Oscar-winning film director John Kinney. Eventually things take an unexpected turn when she lets three young guys move in with her. Romantic comedy, starring Reese Witherspoon, Michael Sheen and Candice Bergen

programme.title: Pacific Rim: Uprising (2018)
programme.desc: Ten years after the Pan Pacific Defense Corps unleashed an army of Jaegers - 25-storey tall robots operated by mind-melded humans - former pilot Jake Pentecost is contacted by estranged sister Mako Mori. She implores him to return to the PPDC to head off the threat posed by Liwen Shao, chief executive of Shao Industries, who intends to replace the Jaegers with unmanned drones. Haunted by ghosts of the past, Jake eventually agrees to mentor a 15-year-old cadet called Amara, whose family were crushed to death during one monster attack. Director Steven S DeKnight's literally blockbusting sci-fi sequel, starring John Boyega, Scott Eastwood and Cailee Spaeny

programme.title: Journeyman (2017)
programme.desc: World middleweight champion Matty Burton is nearing the end of his professional career when he suffers a serious head injury during a fight that leaves him with brain damage. Boxing drama, written, directed by and starring Paddy Considine, with Jodie Whittaker, Paul Popplewell and Tony Pitts

programme.title: American Fable (2016)
programme.desc: When 11-year-old Gitty discovers that her beloved father is hiding a wealthy man in her family's silo in order to save their struggling farm, she is forced to choose between saving the man's life or protecting her family from the consequences of their actions. Thriller, starring Peyton Kennedy, Richard Schiff and Kip Pardue

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Philadelphia to help failing Mexican restaurant Zocalo. Owners Greg and Mary Russell met and fell in love while they were working there, and the place held such sentimental importance to them they pooled their money and bought it. Now Gordon tries to turn things around before the floundering business ruins their marriage and tears their family apart

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The young bakers need to impress judges Prue Leith and Liam Charles with their pastry-plaiting skills, in a challenge that offers the contenders of Heat A their last chance to go through to Finals week. Hosted by Harry Hill

programme.title: Malcolm in the Middle
programme.desc: Lois's disapproving parents come to stay and make a generous offer - before going back on the deal at the last moment. Meanwhile, Francis gets struck down by food poisoning at Mardi Gras. Family comedy, guest starring Cloris Leachman and Robert Loggia, with Frankie Muniz, Jane Kaczmarek and Justin Berfield

programme.title: Malcolm in the Middle
programme.desc: Lois disputes an accusation of reckless driving and orders Francis to pay the $750 in motoring fines she claims he has accumulated in her name. He stages a stunt for the amusement of the other cadets in a bid to raise the money, while Malcolm discovers his mother was wrong to shift blame for the ticket. Comedy, with Bryan Cranston and Jane Kaczmarek

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After more than five years of dating, Sheldon and Amy spend their first night together, while Leonard, Howard and Raj must decide who will take their extra Star Wars movie ticket. Plus, Sheldon's Obi-Wan advises him on his difficult relationship decisions. Starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco, with recurring guest star Bob Newhart

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny gets more than she bargained for when Leonard agrees to meet a psychiatrist on her behalf, while Sheldon and Raj collaborate on an astronomical discovery. Stuart moves out, but Howard and Bernadette's happiness is short-lived. Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco star

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy, focusing on an 11-year-old boy as he captures his family on video camera during the 1980s. Barry wants to start driving on his 16th birthday, but Beverly advises against it and refuses to allow Pops to sell him his old car. Starring Jeff Garlin, Troy Gentile and Wendi McLendon-Covey

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: A day at the roller-skating rink leaves Murray wishing he had not asked Erica to tell him about her life, while Pops steps in to help when a back-to-school shopping trip causes problems between Beverly and Adam. Comedy, starring Jeff Garlin, Wendi McLendon-Covey and Hayley Orrantia

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Diaz try to earn the respect of their idol, Lt Melanie Hawkins after accidentally disrupting one of her undercover cases. Boyle, Jeffords and Gina attempt to resolve a petty conflict between Scully and Hitchcock, while Holt loses Santiago's favourite pen. Guest starring Gina Gershon

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and Diaz learn there's more to Lt Melanie Hawkins' team than meets the eye as they continue their search for the gang of bank robbers. Jeffords, Boyle and Santiago begin to suspect that Gina is harbouring a secret. Ryan Phillippe, Gina Gershon and Jason Mantzoukas guest star

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard turns to Penny for advice when his relationship with Stephanie takes off faster than he would have liked, and he realises he is the last person to notice that she has moved in with him. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's obsession with gift-giving etiquette annoys his friends as Christmas approaches. Meanwhile, Leonard is thrilled to meet an award-winning scientist, only to be filled with jealousy when his new colleague takes an interest in Penny. With guest star Michael Trucco (Battlestar Galactica, One Tree Hill)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: After more than five years of dating, Sheldon and Amy spend their first night together, while Leonard, Howard and Raj must decide who will take their extra Star Wars movie ticket. Plus, Sheldon's Obi-Wan advises him on his difficult relationship decisions. Starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco, with recurring guest star Bob Newhart

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly and Murray fall out over Barry's plan to perform his martial arts routine at the annual high school talent show. Pops buys Erica an electric guitar, urging her to take to the stage and sing. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly complains that Pops is spending too much time with her at home, so Murray takes him to work at the furniture shop. Meanwhile, Adam reluctantly gives away his old toys in an effort to make him look more grown up, hoping it will result in a kiss with Dana. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jeffords' favourite author, DC Parlov, gets in touch after his latest manuscript is stolen, so Jeffords and Peralta convince Diaz to go undercover with them at a fantasy fiction convention to find the culprit. Back at the precinct, Holt, Santiago and Boyle take a forensics course, but Boyle's big mouth gets him in trouble

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: While the squad attends a funeral in Los Angeles, Captain Holt learns that he is in the running for his dream job: Commissioner of the NYPD. however, after they miss their return flight to New York, Peralta must find a way to get Holt back in time for his interview. Santiago tries to convince everyone that she can be laid back, Jeffords panics about losing his first-class flight, and Boyle pries into Diaz's personal life

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Reality experiment in which experts select compatible couples from a long list of applicants, then invite them to marry. It's another week and another explosive commitment ceremony for the couples - shocking behaviour, scandalous truths and devious motives lie in store

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Penny catches the eye of comic-book shopkeeper Stuart, and Leonard is devastated when she gives serious consideration to dating her new admirer. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Comedy, guest starring June Squibb

Beverly refuses to let Adam see a new horror film, but he goes behind her back and tricks Pops into taking him to the cinema. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: The Goldbergs

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: Part two of two. When the warden tells Peralta to investigate Romero's drug smuggling operation, Peralta finds himself trapped by divided loyalties. Meanwhile, Santiago is offered a deal by a local mobster who claims he can prove Lt Hawkins is guilty of the crimes that Diaz and Peralta were jailed for, but Holt and the team think they have found a safer plan. Tim Meadows, Lou Diamond Phillips and Gina Gershon guest star

programme.desc: Now that he's officially back at the precinct and desperate to solve a real criminal case, Peralta finds a loophole to get out of his temporarily designated desk duty. While Boyle provides Peralta with moral support, Jeffords and Santiago help Diaz, who believes Pimento may be cheating on her

Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

Schoolteacher Lauren, who discovered an enormous pond hidden under decking in the back garden, sets out to transform the plot into a beach-themed holiday retreat. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

He also discovers the dark side of the Industrial Revolution and learns how Victorian potteries brought their products to the masses

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Dudley in the West Midlands to Bridgnorth, Shropshire. He learns how Victorian blacksmithing was not for the faint-hearted, rides on one of Britain's most modern trains and traverses the Victoria Bridge at Bewdley in Worcestershire

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts pay a second visit to the Royal Marines Museum in Southsea, near Portsmouth, where members of the public bring along their treasured antiques and collectibles. Items include a Jolly Roger flag flown by the crew of a British submarine during the Second World War, a wine glass that's at the centre of a domestic dispute and a creepy-looking doll with a revolving head and four faces

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn relive the golden age of steam. They begin by exploring how the introduction of steam railways in the early 19th century created a domestic revolution, from the way people lived to the food they ate. Arriving at Ffestiniog Railway in Snowdonia, they find out how millions of tons of slate were moved down the mountain, while underground, Alex experiences the brutal conditions face by miners in the Llechwedd Quarry

programme.desc: Behind the scenes of a car auction business. A near-perfect 50s Chevy panel van and a very desirable low-mileage 80s Brooklands Capri tempt auctioneer Derek out of Yorkshire

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: Projects include a maze of tunnels filled with haunting remains beneath the streets of Paris and a strange formation of domed structures hovering over the waters off Florida

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.desc: Battlefield historians and military experts re-examine some of the Second World War's most iconic sites, beginning with the fate of the island of Malta. Using drones, experts explore the defences of Malta to understand how the island survived

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A floating bridge in Seattle, one of the longest such structures in the world, intended to connect the growing suburb of Bellevue with the high-tech downtown heart of the city. Plus, Albert Zwueste is on the Dutch Caribbean island of Curacao exploring one of the world's oldest surviving floating bridges, which helped inspire and inform the Seattle project

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time

programme.title: Forbidden History

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on the next stage of his journey in which he travels from the naval hub of Portsmouth to Grimsby docks. On the first leg, he helps feed the crew of Britain's newest battleship, discovers how the Victorians planned to repel a possible French invasion and learns there is a well-established industry in an unlikely location

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts pay a second visit to Exeter Cathedral, where more hopeful members of the public bring along treasured antiques and collectibles to hear their stories and find out their worth. Items include Chinese objects that reveal a family secret, artworks by the sculptor Elisabeth Frink, a doll that turns out to be one of the earliest ever featured on the show, and a map of the Battle of Waterloo drawn by an eyewitness

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn continue to relive the golden age of steam and how the Victorian railways created modern Britain. They visit Beamish in County Durham, learning how the rail companies first realised there was money to be made in moving people rather than commodities. Then Peter and Alex live the life of navvies, the men who built the thousands of miles of tracks in all weather conditions, and Ruth finds out what impact the new railroads had on the cottage industries. She also learns about the role of the guard, and the trio learn what precautions were necessary for taking a long journey on a train that was yet to include the necessary facilities

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team struggles to explain how a DAF 33 gearbox works, and office manager Sarah attempts to bring order to the auto memorabilia sale

programme.title: Abandoned Engineering
programme.desc: A town in West Virginia that fell victim to the pace of change when steam trains became obsolete and an underground complex in Italy that had been abandoned for nearly 2,000 years

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length

programme.title: Nazi Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: The theories, conspiracies and facts relating to Rudolf Hess's dramatic flight to Scotland in 1941 and his subsequent death in Spandau prison

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the experts pay a second visit to Exeter Cathedral, where more hopeful members of the public bring along treasured antiques and collectibles to hear their stories and find out their worth. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Woking in Surrey to Clapham Junction in south London. Along the way he gets close to some precious Victorian botany at Kew Gardens, tries his hand at croquet and discovers a very surprising 19th-century place of worship

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Victoria station to Abbey Wood in south-east London. Along the way he learns how volunteer Victorian firefighters liked a tipple, discovers that 19th-century sewage pumps were a marvel of design and puts in a shift at the oldest fish market in Britain

programme.title: The Architecture The Railways Built
programme.desc: Tim Dunn is in London exploring the striking stations of the Piccadilly Line's northern extension. He also takes a look at Causey Arch in Co Durham

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Three MGs from the 70s turn up at the garage, still covered in original factory wax. Nobody can believe it, or value them. A Morris 1000 turns up at the auction along with a TR7 V8

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James looks at some of the advances in motoring that never caught on, revealing how the petrol engine came to dominate in the 20th century, while concepts for jet engines and steam power fell by the wayside. He also drives an amphibious car and an electric scooter, and takes a ride in the world's fastest driverless car

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews return from an overseas holiday to find yet another disaster awaiting them - their home has been demolished. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret agree to pet-sit a tortoise for a friend who ought to know better, and an anniversary outing to the theatre ends in disaster. Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie star

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrew household echoes to the sound of laughter, as Margaret joins the local amateur dramatic society and Victor begins penning a sitcom script. However, his mother-in-law's bath-time accident and a run-in with British Telecom soon leave the curmudgeonly codger in no mood for jokes. Comedy, starring Richard Wilson and Annette Crosbie

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A floating bridge in Seattle, one of the longest such structures in the world, intended to connect the growing suburb of Bellevue with the high-tech downtown heart of the city. Plus, Albert Zwueste is on the Dutch Caribbean island of Curacao exploring one of the world's oldest surviving floating bridges, which helped inspire and inform the Seattle project

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Maritime engineering in recent years has seen ever bigger ships and sea-based platforms constructed. On occasion, they may need moving, which is the purpose of the Boka Vanguard, the planet's largest heavy lift ship, capable of sinking itself, floating under its cargo and then lifting it up to be moved anywhere on the planet with sea. This extraordinary work of engineering is capable of carrying the Eiffel Tower and the Statue of Liberty at the same time

programme.title: Impossible Engineering

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: New York Architect Wendy Fok travels to the remote Guizhou Valley in China to see the Beipanjiang First Bridge, the highest bridge on Earth, which boasts a 20,000 tonne steel deck. She soon discovers that trying to straddle a 565 metre gorge so deep that it's known as the 'crack in the Earth', presented huge challenges for its engineers. Plus, Andrew Steele tracks down a bridge on the thames that nobody thought could ever be built, Suzie Sheehy is in Hampshire climbing the tallest unreinforced concrete town ever built and Luke Bisby scales the Forth Bridge in Edinburgh
